meta.title: Our Roles — couples questionnaire
meta.description: A two-person questionnaire to reveal your expectations, talents and preferences around household management.

hero.subtext: A two-person questionnaire to reveal your expectations, talents and preferences — before the dishes become a battlefield.

book.title: "Profit from the 5 Love Languages"
book.body: The author of The 5 Love Languages observes that most couple conflicts don't stem from big issues like money or family, but from small everyday chores that were never explicitly assigned. This questionnaire uses his method — four angles, six domains — to spare you years of friction.

experience.title: Swipe to answer.
experience.body: Each question comes to life as icons. You swipe the card towards your answer — up, right, down, left — or tap. Faster than a form, more expressive than a list.

feedback.title: Your agreements, and where things chafe.
feedback.body: At the end, you discover your convergences, your divergences, and a suggested distribution based on each person's talents and preferences — not inherited habits.

cta.title: Ready to put words to it?

footer: Inspired by "Profit from the 5 Love Languages" — Gary Chapman.

swipePreview.question: Growing up, who did the cleaning?

create.childrenLabel: Do you have children, or plan to?

join.childrenLabel: Do you have children, or plan to?
